name: Zakuro
message: "It sure is hot..."
The sun on the rooftop was harsh.

name: Zakuro
message: "Maybe I shouldn't have come here..."
I ended up skipping class today too.
Though no one forced me to do it today.
It just happened.
I wasn't really aiming for perfect attendance, but still, once I skipped class a single time, it became a lot easier to do it again.

name: Zakuro
message: "I've never skipped class before..."
I can probably get away with skipping every once in a while.

name: Zakuro
message: "I'm really not used to this, though..."
I came to the rooftop because it didn't seem like anyone else would be up here... and it really was deserted.

name: Zakuro
message: "No one comes up here because it's so hot."
What should I do for an hour?

name: Zakuro
message: "Maybe I should've gone to class after all..."
I put my hand on the fence as I walked across the rooftop.
The school's layout is pretty complicated, so the roof isn't a normal rectangle either.
Building A has a shape like a cross.
The scenery changed as I walked.
The Building B and C rooftops are closed off.

name: Zakuro
message: "Is that..."
Mamiya-kun?
Why?
That's Building B, so it should be off limits. Why is he over there?

name: Zakuro
message: "Huh?"
When I squinted, I could see stairs on the other side of the fence.

name: Zakuro
message: "It's connected to the other building's rooftop here..."
Building B has one less floor, so the stairs lead down.
It doesn't stand out since it's below the lip of the rooftop, but it really is a pretty big staircase.

name: Zakuro
message: "It's probably off limits, but..."

name: Mamiya
message: "Who are you?"
Did he forget about yesterday already?

name: Zakuro
message: "I-I'm Takashima Zakuro. I'm in the next class over."

name: Mamiya
message: "Takashima... Zakuro? That's right."
What does he mean by that?

name: Mamiya
message: "Sorry... I'm really bad at remembering faces."

name: Zakuro
message: "I see..."
But still, it was just yesterday.
I don't really care about him, but it really bothers me that he doesn't even seem to remember me.

name: Mamiya
message: "What are you doing in a place like this?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Oh, uh, nothing in particular."

name: Mamiya
message: "Are you cutting class?"

name: Zakuro
message: "W-Well, I guess so."

name: Mamiya
message: "I see... You shouldn't do that, you know?"

name: Zakuro
message: "B-But you're doing the same thing."

name: Mamiya
message: "I'll be fine."

name: Zakuro
message: "Why?"

name: Mamiya
message: "I always skip classes."

name: Zakuro
message: "Th-That's not a good reason."

name: Mamiya
message: "Who needs a reason? Either way, you shouldn't skip class."

name: Mamiya
message: "Weren't you a good student?"

name: Zakuro
message: "N-Not really..."

name: Mamiya
message: "Oh... Well, if you say so."
What is it...
Yesterday when I left, his voice was so bothersome, but now he almost sounds different.
But his voice hasn't changed...

name: Mamiya
message: "Isn't it nice and cool here?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Eh?"
Now that he mentioned it...
I didn't notice because I was talking to him.
It's pretty cool here.

name: Zakuro
message: "There's shade here, and..."

name: Mamiya
message: "A cool breeze comes out of the forest behind the school."

name: Mamiya
message: "No one comes here because the Building A rooftop is so hot in the summer."

name: Mamiya
message: "Even though the Building C rooftop is so much cooler..."

name: Zakuro
message: "So it's a perfect place for skipping class..."

name: Mamiya
message: "You could say that."

name: Zakuro
message: "What are you doing here, Mamiya-kun?"

name: Mamiya
message: "What does it look like I'm doing?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Reading?"

name: Mamiya
message: "Correct."

name: Zakuro
message: "Is that a light novel?"

name: Mamiya
message: "Do you like light novels?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Not really, but..."

name: Mamiya
message: "This is..."
Mamiya-kun took the cover off the book he was reading.

name: Zakuro
message: "...Cyrano de Bergerac?"

name: Mamiya
message: "It's the most popular play in France..."

name: Zakuro
message: "Popular in France? Is it a modern play?"

name: Mamiya
message: "I wouldn't call it modern... It was written in the 19th century."

name: Zakuro
message: "Is it interesting?"

name: Mamiya
message: "It's incredibly well written, but it seems like it wasn't so well received when it was first published."

name: Zakuro
message: "What do you mean?"

name: Mamiya
message: "I guess you could say it was a heavily commercialized play. To the people of the time, it must have seemed old and musty."

name: Zakuro
message: "I see... It looks like it would be hard to read."

name: Mamiya
message: "It's not that hard. The translation is very recent. Most people who've finished middle school could probably read it."

name: Mamiya
message: "Have you finished middle school?"

name: Zakuro
message: "I suppose I have... Wait, isn't that obvious?"

name: Mamiya
message: "Then you should be able to read it just fine."

name: Zakuro
message: "What kind of story is it?"

name: Mamiya
message: "A poet!"

name: Mamiya
message: "Swordsman!"

name: Mamiya
message: "Scientist!"

name: Mamiya
message: "And a musician!"

name: Mamiya
message: "It's a story about a real person who lived in the 17th century."

name: Zakuro
message: "A true story?"

name: Mamiya
message: "No, it's still a play."

name: Mamiya
message: "It speaks of an ugly man's unrequited love."

name: Zakuro
message: "Unrequited love?"

name: Mamiya
message: "I've lived without knowing the touch of a woman."

name: Zakuro
message: "Eh?"

name: Mamiya
message: "Even my mother thought I was a repugnant child... I had no sisters either... As an adult, women have always looked at me with disdain."

name: Mamiya
message: "It's a play about that person's unrequited pure love."

name: Zakuro
message: "Unrequited... pure love..."

name: Mamiya
message: "That's what it's about."

name: Zakuro
message: "Is it interesting?"

name: Mamiya
message: "Yes, of course. I really like it."
Mamiya-kun laughed kindly.

name: Mamiya
message: "Do you want to read something?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Eh?"

name: Mamiya
message: "Aren't you bored? A lot of the time when I skip class, I read books. Why don't you try one?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Oh, sure..."

name: Mamiya
message: "Would you like to read a light novel?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Ah... What would you recommend?"

name: Mamiya
message: "A light novel?"

name: Zakuro
message: "It doesn't have to be a light novel."

name: Mamiya
message: "What would you prefer?"

name: Zakuro
message: "I don't know a whole lot about that stuff, so I'm not sure, but... I like Alice in Wonderland. I read Through the Looking-Glass, too."

name: Mamiya
message: "Alice! Lewis Carroll, huh? Then how about this?"
Mamiya-kun fished around in his bag and took out a paperback book.

name: Zakuro
message: "What's this?"
There was a cute drawing on the cover, but more than that...

name: Zakuro
message: "Stories Written on a Typewriter... Heh, what's this?"
The title made me laugh...

name: Mamiya
message: "Why don't you try reading it?"
My eyes met his as I took the book from his outstretched hand.
He smiled at me.

name: Zakuro
message: "Th-Thank you..."

name: Mamiya
message: "It's from the same publisher as the one I'm reading, but the words are bigger and the story is easier to understand."

name: Zakuro
message: "Sure..."

name: Mamiya
message: "Light novels are nice too, but sometimes a book like this is better, you know?"

name: Zakuro
message: "B-But I don't read many light novels."

name: Mamiya
message: "Really? You mentioned them earlier, so I thought you liked them."

name: Zakuro
message: "You're the one that likes light novels, aren't you?"

name: Mamiya
message: "I like pretty much all books. I mean..."

name: Zakuro
message: "You mean?"

name: Mamiya
message: "If you just want to hide away in your heart without using any electronics, books are pretty much the best way to do it, aren't they?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Without electronics?"

name: Mamiya
message: "Sadly enough, there aren't any outlets or anything on the roof, so I can't use the Internet or play games up here."

name: Zakuro
message: "Hehehe... You're right."

name: Mamiya
message: "I can't even play games on my cell phone because it's never charged... I'm lazy, so I always forget to charge it."

name: Zakuro
message: "You read instead of playing games because you're lazy?"

name: Mamiya
message: "Something like that. If I wasn't so lazy, I might be able to play games more often."

name: Zakuro
message: "Hehehe... In that case, you might not be skipping class at all."

name: Mamiya
message: "I see... That's a good point."
He's really mysterious...
I opened the book, but Mamiya-kun was distracting me, so I couldn't focus on it.
He interested me far more than the aging pensioners and bald managers in the book.

name: Zakuro
message: "Do you like reading, Mamiya-kun?"

name: Mamiya
message: "Eh? Didn't I say that earlier?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Oh, yeah. You did... Ahahaha."

name: Mamiya
message: "I mostly tend to keep to myself, which is probably why I like books so much."

name: Zakuro
message: "You mostly keep to yourself?"

name: Mamiya
message: "Pretty much."

name: Zakuro
message: "That's the bell."

name: Mamiya
message: "Class is over."

name: Zakuro
message: "Oh, yeah..."

name: Zakuro
message: "Huh?"
"Nii-san..."

name: Zakuro
message: "Nii...san?"

name: Mamiya
message: "What is it? You two don't come up here very often."
You two?
What did he mean by saying, 'you two'?
I only saw one person...
I looked at the girl standing in front of Mamiya-kun.
Does she go to a different school? That's not our uniform...
Though she looks a bit younger than us too.
She must be his little sister.
Putting that aside...
I really don't understand why he said 'you two'...
"...A friend?"
We just happened to both be up here. She's the girl from the next class, uh..."

name: Zakuro
message: "Takashima Zakuro... And you are?"

name: Hasaki
message: "Hasaki... I'm Mamiya Hasaki."

name: Zakuro
message: "Mamiya-kun's little sister?"

name: Zakuro
message: "I see..."
She's really cute.
I don't want to bother them.
She came all the way here to visit him, after all.

name: Zakuro
message: "Well then, I should be going."

name: Mamiya
message: "Oh, sure."

name: Zakuro
message: "Goodbye, Hasaki-san."

name: Hasaki
message: "Bye."

name: Zakuro
message: "Oh, what about this book?"

name: Mamiya
message: "You haven't finished it, right?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Yeah... I-I'm a slow reader."

name: Mamiya
message: "Then keep it until you finish."

name: Mamiya
message: "It's a children's story by a man who wrote a book called The Grammar of Fantasy. It definitely has a lot more to it than most children's books. I think you'll like it."

name: Zakuro
message: "O-Okay..."
I turned and ran off.
What is this feeling? It's like I'm embarrassed, but also very happy. I couldn't stand still, so I ended up dashing off.

name: Kimika
message: "Zakuro? Huh? You skipped again?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Ah, yeah..."

name: Kimika
message: "Hmm, I see... That's rare."
Kimika laughed shortly.
Kimika usually only laughs when she's angry, but this time it seemed different.

name: Megu
message: "Takashima! Let's get something to eat."

name: Zakuro
message: "Oh, okay..."

name: Kimika
message: "Me too..."

name: Satoko
message: "I'm broke."

name: Megu
message: "What do you mean? When did that happen?"

name: Satoko
message: "I'm totally broke... I need some money."

name: Megu
message: "Why don't you have any?"

name: Satoko
message: "I don't know. I never have any money at the end of the month."

name: Megu
message: "But it's not even close to the end of the month."

name: Kimika
message: "But still, her job pays on the 25th, and she gets her allowance on the 30th... This time of month is pretty rough."

name: Satoko
message: "Isn't there an easy way to earn money?"

name: Megu
message: "Don't ask me. If there was, I'd be doing it too."

name: Satoko
message: "Isn't your family stupid rich?"

name: Megu
message: "That doesn't mean I'm rich. My parents only give me 30,000 yen a month for my allowance."

name: Satoko
message: "I'm telling you, that's a ton of money."

name: Megu
message: "How is that a lot of money? I hear that all the girls at that rich kids' school get 100,000 a month."

name: Satoko
message: "Those girls are on a different level, though."

name: Megu
message: "But I don't have any money. It's because I'm always paying for shit for you guys."

name: Satoko
message: "I really appreciate it, though!"

name: Megu
message: "Money, huh?
It's easy if you have someone to give it to you, but earning it yourself is a pain in the ass."

name: Satoko
message: "And I hear you can get in big trouble for selling yourself."

name: Zakuro
message: "What are you selling?"

name: Kimika
message: "Zakuro... Do you have to try to be that stupid, or does it come naturally? You're kind of ticking me off."

name: Zakuro
message: "N-No, I didn't mean to..."

name: Satoko
message: "You're so stupid. I'm talking about prostitution."

name: Megu
message: "But it's really a pain to do, isn't it?"

name: Satoko
message: "Isn't there a more creative way we could earn money?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Creative?"

name: Megu
message: "Isn't there? Haven't you heard? You can even make money blogging and stuff like that."

name: Satoko
message: "You can make money blogging?"

name: Megu
message: "They were talking about it on TV."

name: Satoko
message: "Then why don't we start a blog?"

name: Megu
message: "How?"

name: Satoko
message: "Dunno... I'm not really sure."
Making money with a blog... How would they do that?
No one would care about their blog anyway, since they know nothing about the Internet and have no particular knowledge or skills.

name: Megu
message: "There has to be some way we can earn money fast."

name: Kimika
message: "I don't think there is a way to earn money fast."

name: Megu
message: "Takashima, you draw manga, right?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Ah, yes..."

name: Megu
message: "Can't you make money with that?"

name: Zakuro
message: "I'm not that good... I don't think I could get anything for it."

name: Kimika
message: "Akasaka-san... Why don't you try writing a novel?"

name: Megu
message: "A novel?"

name: Kimika
message: "I heard they're looking for novels. The kind you write on your phone."

name: Megu
message: "Cell phone novels!"

name: Satoko
message: "Yeah, I heard about one that got made into a movie. They're making tons of money."

name: Megu
message: "That's it!"

name: Zakuro
message: "Can you write a novel?"

name: Megu
message: "Won't know 'til I try."

name: Satoko
message: "Anyway, let's try writing one during the next class."

name: Kimika
message: "Anyway, shouldn't we get something to eat? We're running out of time here."

name: Megu
message: "That's right!"

name: Kimika
message: "It's not worth getting that worked up about."
A cell phone novel, huh...
I read part of one before, but it was really strange.
It used lots of weird slang I didn't understand... Actually, I think I read a book that started out kind of like that.
At the beginning, the writer seemed incredibly dumb, but as he got smarter and smarter, the writing got better and more sophisticated.
Though cell phone novels are kind of dumb the whole way through.
What kind of story was that...
Maybe Mamiya-kun has a copy...
There were lots of books in his secret room.
He might have it.
A novel, huh?

name: Zakuro
message: "I ended up borrowing this..."
"Stories Written on a Typewriter."
What a bizarre title. I'd never heard of this before.
When I looked at the table of contents, there were several other short stories included along with the main story.
Each individual story is really short... I guess that makes sense, since children would get bored if they were too long.
I started reading the story from the title and...
Huh... This old man...
He didn't have anywhere to go, so... he turned into a cat.
The old man is the protagonist of the story. He turns into a cat and plays with all the other cats at the beginning of the story.
Hehehe... What a silly story.
It really is the kind of story I like.
It was really interesting.
The author was Gianni Rodari... I'd never heard of him before.
So he knows all about stuff like this.
I've heard of "book sommeliers" before, but does that word really describe someone like Mamiya-kun?
A sommelier is a person who picks out wines to match a customer's tastes... It's just like Mamiya-kun.
I really liked the book Mamiya-kun lent me.
When he gave me this book, he kind of looked like a sommelier offering his finest wine, too.
Though I don't know anything about sommeliers... It seems like it fits.
I stared out the window, lost in thought.

name: Zakuro
message: "I wonder if Mamiya-kun is in his secret room right now."

name: Zakuro
message: "That's right... I should go see him later."

name: Zakuro
message: "He said I could come back whenever I wanted..."

name: Zakuro
message: "I-I hope he doesn't get mad at me."

name: Zakuro
message: "It's pitch black. It's a little scary..."
I waited for a moment until my eyes adjusted.

name: Zakuro
message: "I wish I had a lighter or something..."

name: Zakuro
message: "It's so dark..."
I walked along the wall.
Huh?

name: Zakuro
message: "This..."
There were scribbled red letters hanging in the dark.
The letters scrawled childishly on the wall said...

name: Zakuro
message: "Why aren't you a virgin?"

name: Zakuro
message: "Die, you slut."
When I looked closer, there were scribbles all over the walls of the reservoir tank.
They were all...

name: Zakuro
message: "Kind of..."

name: Zakuro
message: "A little scary."
I quickly left the reservoir tank.
Why...
He seemed so intelligent before.
Why would he write that?

name: Mamiya
message: "Ah, Takashima-san!"

name: Mamiya
message: "Wh-Why are you here? Were you in my secret room?"

name: Mamiya
message: "H-Hey!"

name: Mamiya
message: "What's wrong?"
I ran from that place.
It was kind of scary.
Mamiya-kun seems like a different person when he's here.

name: Zakuro
message: "Hmm?"
I ate dinner at a silent table and returned to my room.
I didn't have anything in particular to do, so I opened my phone to get on the Internet.

name: Zakuro
message: "Another message..."
It must be another message from some random guy.
I felt depressed as I opened my messages.

name: Zakuro
message: "Ugh... The person from yesterday."
"I saw you didn't accept my friend request yet. Did I say something to offend you?"
"I'd like to apologize if I did."
No, not really. That's not it...
I went ahead and accepted his request.
I don't like to accept friend requests from people I don't know, but I had no choice.

name: Zakuro
message: "Huh?"
There was another message... Were there two of them?
I opened the other message.
from: Usami
Subject: I've finally made contact with you.
I'm not sure if I should say... nice to meet you.
"The battle at the stone tower."
Please respond when you remember the significance of these words.
Also, you will probably experience a large change.
It will probably be a good thing.

name: Zakuro
message: "What... is this?"
The battle at the stone tower...
Is that from a game... or maybe a novel? Or maybe even a manga?
Why would that phrase be significant? I've never heard that before.
Do I just naturally attract weird people like this?
Maybe it's because I'm so gloomy...
Maybe they can tell just by reading my journal online...

name: Zakuro
message: "Ah... This is the worst."
I should just go to sleep.
Hmph.
I love the sound that comes out when I fall into bed.
The sheets always feel good, too.
I put my blankets in the drier while I was eating.
It makes a big difference.
That's probably a good thing.

name: Zakuro
message: "They felt sweaty yesterday, but now they're nice and soft."
That's the only "large change" that could happen in my life.
Especially if we're talking about good things that could happen.

name: Zakuro
message: "Though there have been plenty of bad changes."
Oh well...
Time to sleep.
Ah...
I have to brush my teeth...
And wash my face.
A black shadow... draped over the sky.
An dreary black shadow that stretches into the infinite.
Like the world's twilight...
A dreary scene.
A dream I've never had before.
But one that seems so familiar.
Maybe... it was a dream from long ago.
The dream made me sad...
Then it disappeared.
